cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the order of these books. There are five books: white, green, brown, gray, and orange. They're arranged in a fixed order on a shelf. I need to use the clues given to determine their positions. First, let me list out all the clues again to make sure I don't miss any: 1. The gray book is to the right of the orange book. 2. The green book is the second from the right. 3. The brown book is to the right of the white book. 4. The brown book is to the left of the orange book. Let me start by considering the positions from left to right as positions 1 to 5. So, position 1 is the leftmost, and position 5 is the rightmost. Clue 2 says the green book is the second from the right. That means position 4 is green. So, let me note that down: Position 4: Green Now, clue 1: Gray is to the right of orange. So wherever orange is, gray has to be somewhere to its right (i.e., a higher position number). Clue 4: Brown is to the left of orange. So brown comes before orange. Clue 3: Brown is to the right of white. So white comes before brown. So putting clues 3 and 4 together: white is before brown, and brown is before orange. So the order must be white, then brown,
